_comment: Source of truth for all work/case study cards. Edit here; index.html and work.html render from this file.

cards.title: When the process is the problem
cards.description: A multi-vendor Microsoft Surface web program running on manual spreadsheets, ad-hoc handoffs, and a shared QA tracker twenty people were about to use simultaneously. Five broken systems. A three-horizon remediation roadmap.
cards.workDescription: A partner-heavy web program running on manual spreadsheets, color-coded Word docs, and ad-hoc handoffs. Mapped five broken delivery systems and built a three-horizon remediation roadmap.

cards.title: Enterprise at a human scale
cards.description: Four years delivering digital programs across a regulated healthcare system — a secure physician portal, patient scheduling, a Sitecore migration, and the process infrastructure that held it all together.
cards.workDescription: Led digital strategy across a regulated healthcare system — secure physician portals, online scheduling, a Sitecore migration, and the process infrastructure that held it all together.

cards.title: Teaching AI to help, not replace
cards.description: Platform strategy approved by the CTO and CMO. Delivery infrastructure built from scratch. AI workflows running across content, architecture decisions, and requirements — practical, not performative.
cards.workDescription: Platform strategy approved by the CTO and CMO. Delivery infrastructure built from scratch. AI workflows cutting recurring content production time to a fraction — practical, not performative.

cards.title: Building an agency from the inside
cards.description: A fee-for-service internal digital agency for Texas school boards — 40+ hours of stakeholder focus groups, a full measurement chain from GTM through Power BI, and a headless CMS migration alongside it all.
cards.workDescription: Ran a fee-for-service internal digital agency for Texas school boards — scopes, budgets, 40+ hours of stakeholder focus groups, and an analytics model connecting member behavior to business goals.

cards.title: 100,000 tasks and counting
cards.description: Two visibility layers for a Texas K-12 statewide assessment PMO: MS Project customization so PMs could see the portfolio, Power BI so executives could. The infrastructure problem underneath both.
cards.workDescription: Managed interconnected project schedules at enterprise scale for Texas K-12 statewide assessment — and built the two-layer visibility infrastructure that made 100,000 tasks legible to executives.

cards.title: Digital access as a public good
cards.description: Three beats: a website rebuilt from scratch, a learning management system built from Google Sheets and Forms, and a PRSA-winning campaign that quadrupled traffic to a statewide civil legal help platform.
cards.workDescription: Rebuilt a nonprofit web presence, built a learning management system from Google Sheets and Forms, and ran a PRSA-winning campaign that quadrupled traffic to TexasLawHelp.org.
